last_result.proof_state: The committed EasyCrypt proof state was not changed.

source.label: Parsed from EasyCrypt pretty goal text

navigation.confidence_reason: The current pure goal exposes relevant list/local symbols, but the goal is large or the local evidence is partial.
navigation.why_now: current ambient goal mentions list/subtype/local conversion operators that often close after unfolding the local head and supplying qualified lemma names.

preconditions: Use only when the visible goal is a pure ambient/list obligation.
Lookup any non-trivial cross-file lemma before using it if its signature is unknown.
Probe the block; if it fails, shorten to the intro/unfold prefix.
fast_track_probe.expected_next_shape: The pure list/subtype obligation closes, or the remaining goal points to one missing local lemma/unfold.

anti_routes.reason: Blind SMT often misses cloned/local qualified names.
Prefer the qualified/local name shown by lookup or lemma_hints.

repair_if_fails.next: inspect lemma_hints or lookup_symbol for the missing qualified name
try a smaller unfold/rewrite prefix before changing proof family

visible_destructors.effect: Separates a membership fact into left-source and right-source cases.

visible_destructors.limitations: Predicate simplification is left to checked local proof steps.

visible_destructors.effect: Splits list membership into head and tail cases.

visible_destructors.effect: Keeps finite-map update keys visible beside membership obligations.

limitations: No destructor tactic is chosen by this surface.

inspect_lookup_handles.effect: All handles here are read-only manager requests; the committed EasyCrypt proof state stays unchanged.

ask_manager_for.use_when: The latest transition recorded an error.

Need local or standard-library lemma names before rewriting a pure goal.
ask_manager_for.returns: nearby lemma hints and qualified-name lookup guidance

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need the valid EasyCrypt form for rewrite, apply, conseq, while, or SMT setup.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid tactic forms and common traps

items.label: After seq opened / before branch work #11
items.committed_tactic: sp; rnd; wp; while (={p2, c2, i, n, a, p0, p, Mem.log, Mem.lc, BNR.lenc, BNR.ndec, UFCMA.log, UFCMA.cbad1, glob ROIN.RO, glob ROout, glob ROF} /\ inv_lbad1 UFCMA_l.lbad1{2} BNR.lenc{2} UFCMA.log{2} Mem.log{2} Mem.lc{2} UFCMA.cbad1{2} BNR.ndec{2} /\ (UFCMA.bad1{1} <=> exists (tt:tag*tag), (tt \in UFCMA_l.lbad1{2}) /\ tt.`1 = tt.`2) /\ check_plaintext BNR.lenc{1} p{1} /\ n{1} = (p{1}).`1); auto.
items.why_checkpoint: seq-local branch boundary; selecting it keeps the seq cut and removes branch-local work after it
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #11 and every committed tactic after it in this node.

items.label: Before seq cut #10
items.committed_tactic: seq 12 12 : (={n, a, c1, lt, t0, p0, p, Mem.log, Mem.lc, BNR.lenc, BNR.ndec, UFCMA.log, UFCMA.cbad1, glob ROIN.RO, glob ROout, glob ROF} /\ inv_lbad1 UFCMA_l.lbad1{2} BNR.lenc{2} UFCMA.log{2} Mem.log{2} Mem.lc{2} UFCMA.cbad1{2} BNR.ndec{2} /\ (UFCMA.bad1{1} <=> exists (tt:tag*tag), (tt \in UFCMA_l.lbad1{2}) /\ tt.`1 = tt.`2) /\ check_plaintext BNR.lenc{1} p{1} /\ n{1} = (p{1}).`1 /\ lt{2} = map (fun (c3:ciphertext) => c3.`4) (filter (fun (c3:ciphertext) => c3.`1 = n{2}) Mem.lc{2})).
